description_long: This is a rustic, earthenware vase with a bulbous body and a wide, uneven mouth. The surface is mottled with various shades of brown and tan, featuring subtle speckles and natural imperfections that suggest it was hand-formed from clay. The opening is irregular and slightly chipped, giving it an aged, weathered appearance. The body tapers gently toward the base, which is not visible but implied to be flat for stability. The interior is dark and unglazed, contrasting with the rough, matte exterior. This vase exudes a utilitarian charm, reminiscent of traditional pottery often found in outdoor urban gardens or courtyards.
description: A weathered, brown earthenware vase with a wide, uneven mouth and a rough, speckled surface. Its rustic appearance suggests hand-crafted clay construction.
description_view_0: The wide, open mouth of the vase is visible, showing the dark, hollow interior and the irregular, chipped rim.
description_view_1: The side profile highlights the bulbous, rounded body and the natural, mottled texture of the clay surface.
description_view_2: The base of the vase is shown, appearing flat and unadorned, with the body tapering inward.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the vase is visible, displaying a darker, more shadowed surface with subtle textural variations.
